_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what changed.

### Change
In `tsconfig.json`, `"strict": true` was added under `compilerOptions`.

### Why
`strict` turns on TypeScript’s strict checking bundle, including things like `strictNullChecks`, `noImplicitAny`, `strictFunctionTypes`, and `strictBindCallApply`, so the compiler catches more unsafe patterns.

### File touched
- `/home/shubham/Streamlist/StreamList/tsconfig.json`

Because this project extends `@react-native/typescript-config`, your effective options are the base config plus this override. After pulling or opening the project, run your usual typecheck (`tsc` or your app’s script); you may see new errors where code relied on non-strict behavior—those are expected and worth fixing over time.

_**Agent (default)**_


Here is what is set up and what you need to do locally.

### Already done
- **Git** was already initialized (you had an initial commit on `main`).
- **`origin`** is set to [https://github.com/modishubham/streamlist.git](https://github.com/modishubham/streamlist.git).
- **Committed** your strict-mode change: `Enable TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig` (updates `tsconfig.json`).

### Push from your machine
Pushing from this environment failed because GitHub needs your credentials (no interactive login here). On your own computer, in the project folder, run:

```bash
git push -u origin main
```

Then sign in when prompted, or use one of these approaches:

- **HTTPS + credential helper / browser** — usual GitHub Desktop or `gh auth login` flow.
- **Personal access token** — when asked for a password, use a [GitHub PAT](https://github.com/settings/tokens) with `repo` scope.
- **SSH** — if you use SSH keys with GitHub:
  ```bash
  git remote set-url origin git@github.com:modishubham/streamlist.git
  git push -u origin main
  ```

After a successful push, the repo at [modishubham/streamlist](https://github.com/modishubham/streamlist) will show your code.

### Untracked items (not committed)
`.specstory/` and `.vscode/` are still untracked. Add them to `.gitignore` if you do not want them in the repo, or commit them if you do. Say if you want a suggested `.gitignore` snippet for those.
